title: Programs
subtitle: Structured ways to contribute, learn, partner, mentor, fund, and steward open work.
audience: Potential volunteers, fellows, mentors, partners, funders, and open-source contributors.
lede: Institute programs turn interest into participation through volunteer, internship, mentorship, fellowship, partnership, philanthropy, grants, and open-source pathways.

sections.heading: Volunteer
sections.body: Volunteering is the broadest on-ramp for participation. People can contribute to learning groups, projects, operations, writing, analysis, media, and community support according to available time, background, and skills.

sections.heading: Internship and Mentorship
sections.body: Internships and mentorship provide more structured engagement. These pathways help participants learn by contributing to projects, public materials, educational work, research workflows, and community operations.

sections.heading: Fellows
sections.body: The Fellowship pathway supports contributors who provide regular progress reports and develop research, applications, or public resources aligned with the Institute and ecosystem.

sections.heading: Partnership and Philanthropy
sections.body: Partners and donors support shared research, public education, infrastructure, and project development. Partnership is a practical exchange of time, attention, expertise, funding, and institutional support.

sections.heading: Open Source
sections.body: Open source is an ecosystem support function: publish reusable work, improve project infrastructure, and make shared technical resources discoverable.

cards.title: Volunteer
cards.text: Contribute to learning groups, project support, operations, writing, analysis, or media.

cards.title: Internship
cards.text: Join a structured contribution pathway with learning, documentation, and project practice.

cards.title: Mentorship
cards.text: Support participant development and project continuity.

cards.title: Fellowship
cards.text: Develop research or applied work with regular reporting and public outputs.

cards.title: Partnership
cards.text: Align organizations, funders, and collaborators around concrete shared work.

cards.title: Philanthropy
cards.text: Support public education, research infrastructure, events, and ecosystem stewardship.
